report_metadata.report_title: PRC State-Sponsored Cyber Actor Living off the Land to Evade Detection

yara_rules.name: CustomFRPClient
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule CustomFRPClient { meta: description=\"Identify instances of the actor's custom FRP tool...\"

yara_rules.name: HACKTOOL_FRPClient
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule HACKTOOL_FRPClient { meta: description=\"Identify instances of FRP tool (Note: This tool is known to be used by multiple actors, so hits would not necessarily imply activity by the specific actor described in this report)\"

ttps.evidence_snippet: The actor takes measures to hide their tracks, such as clearing logs [T1070.001].

summary: This advisory provides an overview of hunting guidance and associated best practices to detect activity associated with a People’s Republic of China (PRC) state-sponsored cyber actor, also known as Volt Typhoon.

timeline.event: First known exploitation of CVE-2021-40539 (ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus)
timeline.evidence_snippet: Earliest evidence of exploitation of CVE-2021-40539 occurred on May 27, 2023, when threat actors began targeting ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus installations.
